prompt:
Design a Workforce Learning & Procurement Reconciliation Portal to let organizations manage employee learning paths alongside vendor order governance and settlement in a fully offline environment. Users include Learners, Procurement Specialists, Approvers, Finance Analysts, Content Moderators, and System Administrators. The React web UI provides local username/password sign-in, optional MFA via time-based one-time passwords generated in an authenticator app, and role-based menus that only reveal permitted pages and actions. Learners can search a unified library of internal resources and job/skill tags using fast on-site full-text search with typo tolerance, optional synonym and pinyin matching, and multi-dimensional filters for date, category, and tags; results can be sorted by relevance, popularity, or most recent, with clear “why recommended” cues. The experience supports intelligent recommendations driven by viewing/completion behavior and tags, including cold-start defaults by job family, deduplication across near-duplicate resources, and diversity controls that ensure no more than 40% of a recommendation carousel comes from a single category. Learners enroll in learning paths with required/elective rules (for example, 6 required items plus any 2 electives) and track progress down to each resource, with resume learning, cross-device sync within the same offline network, and export of learning records to a downloadable CSV. Procurement and Finance users review orders and disputes through a guided workflow: ratings with text and image attachments, merchant replies, negative-review appeals with evidence uploads, and arbitration outcomes that control whether a review is hidden, shown with a disclaimer, or restored.

The backend uses Go with Echo to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the decoupled React frontend, and PostgreSQL persists all business data locally, including users, roles/permissions, learning paths, resource progress events, job/skill taxonomy, reviews, disputes, billing rules, and settlement ledgers. Search indexing is built and maintained on-premise: tokenization, weighting, and fuzzy match are executed locally, with nightly rebuilds and incremental updates on content changes; archive pages are auto-generated by month and tag based on persisted publish dates. The job/skill taxonomy supports hierarchical relationships, synonym consolidation, and conflict detection rules (for example, blocking two active synonyms pointing to different canonical tags), with an admin review queue and audit trails. Reconciliation supports configurable warehouse and transportation billing rules, generation of AR/AP entries, statement comparison, variance write-off thresholds (for example, variances under $5.00 auto-suggest write-off but still require Finance approval), settlement status transitions, and cost allocation by department and cost center, with exports compatible with downstream finance systems via offline file drop or LAN-based webhooks. Security includes salted password hashing, encryption at rest for sensitive fields (such as recovery secrets and dispute evidence metadata), masking of personal data in non-admin views, configurable session timeouts (15 minutes idle, 8 hours max), and a centralized configuration center with feature flags, parameters, phased rollout by user role, scheduled jobs with retry/compensation, and app version compatibility checks that block unsupported clients while allowing read-only access for up to 14 days.
